description: People may greet, share food, or arrange a gathering in a way their group recognises as usual. That recurring social practice is 'keno'. A 'therema' (tradition) is handed from one generation to the next, while 'thole' (practice) emphasises deliberate repetition that develops capacity.
articulatory_notes: Seal the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into stressed middle-height front 'e'. For dental 'n', place the tongue tip at the upper teeth and let voiced air pass through the nose. Round the lips to finish on middle-height back 'o'.
usage_notes: Phi places the relevant person or community before the noun, shown by 'sila keno' (community custom). A custom may be recent, disputed, changing, or harmful; calling it usual does not grant it authority. A source label may identify the particular custom when a translation would blur it.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Repeated daily acts can preserve practical memory. They can also preserve an old mistake, so inheritance and examination belong in the same conversation.

semantic_domains.activity: a practice repeated as an ordinary way of acting
semantic_domains.community: a social group treating a pattern as its customary way
